findings.claim: Q1 distractor A is labeled 'reversed_causality' in generator_meta.planted_traps, but the trap actually functions as plausible_worldknowledge / contradiction of a stated non-factor, not a cause-effect inversion.
findings.evidence: Passage: 'Inte skribentens utbildning, visade det sig, utan att hon eller han år efter år iakttog exakt samma sak på samma sätt'. Option A: 'Att skribenten hade en biologisk utbildning att luta sig mot när noteringarna gjordes.' A asserts a factor the text explicitly rejected (education plausibly seems important in the real world); it does not swap a cause and an effect. The Q1 rationale itself hedges with a dual label ('A är reversed_causality/plausible_worldknowledge'), which is inconsistent with the single 'reversed_causality' label committed in generator_meta.
findings.why: Integrated review already returned MINOR_NOTES, and this imprecise/dual trap label is a plausible instance of what that verdict covers. The trap still functions correctly (A is genuinely wrong and tempting), so it does not overturn any stage verdict — a real-but-minor labeling observation, not a stage-changing defect.
